query: What tyre degradation problems did drivers report over the weekend?
messages: VER: Front-left is graining already, losing the front end into turn 4.
VER: Rear tyres are gone after eight laps, big degradation on the hard compound.
LEC: Front-left graining is back, I'm struggling to hold this pace.
expected_output: Verstappen reported front-left graining in FP1 and heavy rear degradation on the hard tyre in FP2. Leclerc suffered front-left graining again during the race, hurting his pace.
assertions: Reports the front-left graining described in the radio messages
Attributes a tyre problem to Verstappen, consistent with the messages
Describes only tyre issues that appear in the messages

query: Did McLaren use an undercut, and did it work?
messages: Engineer to LEC: Box this lap, box box, we go to the hard tyre.
Engineer to NOR: Leclerc is pitting, this is the undercut window, push now.
Engineer to NOR: Undercut worked, you came out ahead of Leclerc, P2 now.
expected_output: Yes. When Leclerc pitted, McLaren told Norris to push in the undercut window. It worked and Norris came out ahead of Leclerc into P2.
assertions: States the undercut outcome the way the messages describe it (it worked)
Says Norris gained the position, consistent with the messages
Refers only to drivers named in the messages

query: Why were lap times deleted in qualifying?
messages: NOR: Yellow flag in sector 2, I had to lift, lap is deleted.
LEC: Lap time deleted for track limits at turn 9, I ran wide.
expected_output: Two laps were deleted: Norris had to lift for a yellow flag in sector 2, and Leclerc lost a lap for exceeding track limits at turn 9.
assertions: Gives the yellow flag as a reason, per the messages
Gives track limits as a reason, per the messages
Does not add a reason that is not in the messages

query: What happened with the weather during the race?
messages: VER: It's starting to rain in sector 3, light drizzle on my visor.
Engineer to PIA: Rain expected to intensify in five minutes, prepare for intermediates.
HAM: Conditions are treacherous, no grip on the slicks, I nearly lost it at turn 4.
expected_output: Light rain began in sector 3 and was expected to intensify, prompting teams to prepare intermediates. Conditions became treacherous on slicks, with Hamilton nearly losing the car at turn 4.
assertions: States that rain affected the race, per the messages
Mentions preparing intermediate tyres, as the messages say
Does not claim the race stayed dry

query: Was there a safety car and what triggered it?
messages: LEC: Safety car is out, there's a car stopped at turn 6.
Engineer to NOR: Safety car this lap, box for intermediates, it's the right call.
expected_output: Yes. A safety car was deployed for a car stopped at turn 6, and teams used it to pit for intermediates.
assertions: Confirms a safety car, as stated in the messages
Gives the stopped car at turn 6 as the trigger, per the messages
Does not invent a different cause

query: Did any driver have brake problems?
messages: LEC: Brakes are running hot, I can smell them on the long straight.
expected_output: Leclerc reported his brakes running hot in FP1, to the point he could smell them on the long straight.
assertions: Attributes the brake issue to Leclerc, per the messages
Describes the brakes overheating or running hot, as the messages state

query: Were there team orders at Mercedes?
messages: HAM: I have DRS on Russell, I'll have a look down the inside into turn 1.
Engineer to RUS: Hamilton has DRS, hold position, do not fight your team-mate.
expected_output: Yes. Hamilton had DRS on Russell and intended to attack into turn 1; Mercedes told Russell to hold position and not fight his team-mate.
assertions: Reports the Mercedes hold-position or no-fight instruction from the messages
Refers to both Hamilton and Russell, as named in the messages

query: Who won the race and how did they manage the lead?
messages: Engineer to VER: P1 provisional, that's pole, great job.
Engineer to VER: Good launch, you keep the lead into turn 1.
Engineer to VER: Last lap, gap is four seconds, bring it home, you've won it.
expected_output: Verstappen won. He took pole, kept the lead off the line into turn 1, managed the gap, and brought it home with a four-second margin on the last lap.
assertions: Names the winner the radio messages indicate (Verstappen, who took pole and was told he had won)
Notes he led from pole or from the start, per the messages
Does not name a winner the messages do not support
